description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages tests, build, deploy and release using the mxklb/docker-qt5-deb Docker image and a global before_script that makes gitlab.sh executable and runs it. Define a .build_appimage template that runs on the xenial image, executes "qmake cuteproject.pro -r CONFIG+=release" then "make", then in after_script makes pkgs/version.sh and pkgs/appimage.sh executable, runs pkgs/appimage.sh and moves the generated cuteproject-* files, publishing them as an artifact named cuteproject-x86_64; define a .build_debian_pkg template that runs "chmod +x pkgs/version.sh", "chmod +x pkgs/debian.sh && pkgs/debian.sh" and moves pkgs/deb/cuteproject-*.deb, storing the .deb files as artifacts. Add a .debug_build_with_tests template that runs only when $CI_COMMIT_TAG is null, executes "qmake cuteproject.pro -r CONFIG+=debug" and "make"; create jobs appimage_latest (extends .build_appimage), appimage_stable (extends .build_appimage, runs only on tag commits, stage release), debian_trusty_latest (extends .build_debian_pkg, uses mxklb/docker-qt5-deb:trusty, sets USER=$GITLAB_USER_LOGIN, artifact name cuteproject-trusty_amd64), debian_trusty_stable (extends previous, runs on tag commits, stage release), debian_xenial_latest (extends .build_debian_pkg, uses mxklb/docker-qt5-deb:xenial, artifact name cuteproject-xenial_amd64), debian_xenial_stable (extends previous, runs on tag commits, stage release), debug_tests_coverage (extends .debug_build_with_tests, after_script runs gcovr and lcov.sh, moves coverage PDFs and tarballs, and publishes them as test_coverage artifacts), debug_tests_devel (extends .debug_build_with_tests, allow_failure, uses mxklb/docker-qt5-deb:devel), debug_tests_rolling (extends .debug_build_with_tests, allow_failure, uses mxklb/docker-qt5-deb:rolling), release_build (stage build, before_script runs gitlab.sh and writes "catchTests=false" to globals.pri, then runs the release qmake and make commands), and release_tests (stage tests, runs the same release qmake and make script).
